Tetouan City Power Consumption Analysis¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import seaborn as sns
import sys
sys.path.append('.')
from config import *

plt.rcParams.update({
    'figure.dpi': 150,
    'axes.spines.top': False,
    'axes.spines.right': False,
})

Exploratory Data Analysis¶

Split¶

In [6]:
train = df[df.index < SPLIT_DATE]
test  = df[df.index >= SPLIT_DATE]

print(f'Train: {len(train):,}  ({train.index.min().date()} → {train.index.max().date()})')
print(f'Test:  {len(test):,}  ({test.index.min().date()} → {test.index.max().date()})')

Train: 43,776  (2017-01-01 → 2017-10-31)
Test:  8,640  (2017-11-01 → 2017-12-30)

Feature Distributions¶

In [7]:
def plot_distribution(feature):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
    sns.histplot(train[feature], ax=ax, color='#2166ac', alpha=0.6, label='train')
    sns.histplot(test[feature],  ax=ax, color='#d6604d', alpha=0.6, label='test')
    ax.set_title(feature)
    ax.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

for feature in df.columns:
    plot_distribution(feature)

Autocorrelation (ACF / PACF)¶

In [9]:
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf

fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
for i, zone in enumerate(ZONES):
    plot_acf(df[zone], lags=200, ax=axes[i][0], title=f'ACF — {ZONE_LABELS[i]}')
    plot_pacf(df[zone], lags=50, ax=axes[i][1], title=f'PACF — {ZONE_LABELS[i]}', method='ywm')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Feature Engineering¶

In [10]:
def build_features(df, target_col, horizon=1):
    feat = df[WEATHER].copy()

    feat['hour'] = df.index.hour
    feat['weekday'] = df.index.weekday
    feat['month'] = df.index.month
    feat['is_weekend'] = (df.index.weekday >= 5).astype(int)
    feat['hour_sin'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * df.index.hour / 24)
    feat['hour_cos'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * df.index.hour / 24)
    feat['month_sin'] = np.sin(2 * np.pi * df.index.month / 12)
    feat['month_cos'] = np.cos(2 * np.pi * df.index.month / 12)

    target = df[target_col]
    for lag in [1, 2, 144, 1008]:
        actual_lag = max(lag, horizon)
        feat[f'lag_{lag}'] = target.shift(actual_lag)

    for window in [6, 12, 144]:
        feat[f'roll_mean_{window}'] = target.shift(horizon).rolling(window).mean()
        feat[f'roll_std_{window}'] = target.shift(horizon).rolling(window).std()

    feat[target_col] = target
    result = feat.dropna()
    print(f'{target_col} (horizon={horizon}): {result.shape}')
    return result
In [11]:
feat_z1 = build_features(df, ZONES[0])
feat_z2 = build_features(df, ZONES[1])
feat_z3 = build_features(df, ZONES[2])
print(feat_z1.columns.tolist())

Modeling¶

Split¶

In [12]:
def split_features(feat, target_col):
    X_train = feat[feat.index < SPLIT_DATE].drop(columns=[target_col])
    X_test  = feat[feat.index >= SPLIT_DATE].drop(columns=[target_col])
    y_train = feat[feat.index < SPLIT_DATE][target_col]
    y_test  = feat[feat.index >= SPLIT_DATE][target_col]
    print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}  Test: {X_test.shape}')
    return X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test
In [13]:
X_train_z1, X_test_z1, y_train_z1, y_test_z1 = split_features(feat_z1, ZONES[0])
X_train_z2, X_test_z2, y_train_z2, y_test_z2 = split_features(feat_z2, ZONES[1])
X_train_z3, X_test_z3, y_train_z3, y_test_z3 = split_features(feat_z3, ZONES[2])

Train: (42768, 23)  Test: (8640, 23)
Train: (42768, 23)  Test: (8640, 23)
Train: (42768, 23)  Test: (8640, 23)

ARIMA Baseline¶

In [14]:
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error
from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA

def rmse(y_true, y_pred):
    return np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred))

def mape(y_true, y_pred):
    return np.mean(np.abs((y_true - y_pred) / y_true)) * 100

def train_arima(zone, label):
    model = ARIMA(hourly_train[zone], order=(2, 1, 2)).fit()
    pred = model.forecast(steps=len(hourly_test))
    result = {
        'rmse': rmse(hourly_test[zone], pred),
        'mape': mape(hourly_test[zone].values, pred.values),
    }
    print(f'{label}: RMSE={result["rmse"]:.1f}  MAPE={result["mape"]:.2f}%')
    return result

hourly = df[ZONES].resample('h').mean()
hourly_train = hourly[hourly.index < SPLIT_DATE]
hourly_test  = hourly[hourly.index >= SPLIT_DATE]

arima_results = {}
for zone, label in zip(ZONES, ZONE_LABELS):
    arima_results[label] = train_arima(zone, label)

Zone 1: RMSE=6643.5  MAPE=21.88%
Zone 2: RMSE=5537.7  MAPE=21.11%
Zone 3: RMSE=3350.2  MAPE=26.91%

XGBoost¶

In [15]:
from xgboost import XGBRegressor

def train_xgb(X_tr, y_tr, X_te, y_te, n_estimators=1000, early_stopping_rounds=50):
    model = XGBRegressor(
        n_estimators=n_estimators,
        learning_rate=0.05,
        max_depth=6,
        subsample=0.8,
        colsample_bytree=0.8,
        early_stopping_rounds=early_stopping_rounds,
        random_state=42,
        n_jobs=-1,
    )
    model.fit(X_tr, y_tr, eval_set=[(X_te, y_te)], verbose=False)
    pred = model.predict(X_te)
    print(f'RMSE: {rmse(y_te, pred):.1f}  MAPE: {mape(y_te.values, pred):.2f}%')
    return model, pred
In [16]:
model_z1, pred_z1 = train_xgb(X_train_z1, y_train_z1, X_test_z1, y_test_z1)
model_z2, pred_z2 = train_xgb(X_train_z2, y_train_z2, X_test_z2, y_test_z2)
model_z3, pred_z3 = train_xgb(X_train_z3, y_train_z3, X_test_z3, y_test_z3)

RMSE: 398.3  MAPE: 0.98%
RMSE: 297.5  MAPE: 0.86%
RMSE: 401.3  MAPE: 2.51%

Actual vs Predicted¶

In [18]:
def plot_predictions(y_test, pred, label):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 3))
    ax.plot(y_test.index[:2016], y_test.values[:2016], label='Actual', color='#2166ac', linewidth=0.8)
    ax.plot(y_test.index[:2016], pred[:2016], label='Predicted', color='#d6604d', linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.85)
    ax.set_title(f'{label} — Actual vs Predicted (first 2 weeks)')
    ax.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_predictions(y_test_z1, pred_z1, 'Zone 1')
plot_predictions(y_test_z2, pred_z2, 'Zone 2')
plot_predictions(y_test_z3, pred_z3, 'Zone 3')

Interpretability¶

SHAP Feature Importance¶

In [19]:
import shap

def plot_shap(model, X_test, label):
    explainer = shap.TreeExplainer(model)
    shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test)
    shap.summary_plot(shap_values, X_test, plot_type='bar', max_display=15, show=False)
    plt.gcf().set_size_inches(6, 4)
    plt.title(f'SHAP — {label}')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    return shap_values

shap_z1 = plot_shap(model_z1, X_test_z1, 'Zone 1')
shap_z2 = plot_shap(model_z2, X_test_z2, 'Zone 2')
shap_z3 = plot_shap(model_z3, X_test_z3, 'Zone 3')

Forecasting Horizon Analysis¶

In [20]:
def build_horizon_features(horizon):
    feats = {}
    for zone in ZONES:
        feats[zone] = build_features(df, zone, horizon)
    return feats

def train_horizon(horizon_feats, zone):
    feat = horizon_feats[zone]
    X_tr, X_te, y_tr, y_te = split_features(feat, zone)
    model, pred = train_xgb(X_tr, y_tr, X_te, y_te, n_estimators=500, early_stopping_rounds=30)
    return {'rmse': rmse(y_te, pred), 'mape': mape(y_te.values, pred)}
In [21]:
horizon_results = {label: {} for label in ZONE_LABELS}

for name, h in HORIZONS.items():
    feats = build_horizon_features(h)
    for zone, label in zip(ZONES, ZONE_LABELS):
        horizon_results[label][name] = train_horizon(feats, zone)
    print(f'--- {name} done ---')

In [22]:
def plot_horizon(horizon_results, label='Zone 1'):
    horizon_df = pd.DataFrame(horizon_results).T

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
    for ax, metric, ylabel in zip(axes, ['rmse', 'mape'], ['RMSE', 'MAPE (%)']):
        ax.plot(range(len(horizon_df)), horizon_df[metric], marker='o', color=PALETTE[0])
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(horizon_df)))
        ax.set_xticklabels(horizon_df.index, rotation=30, ha='right')
        ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
        ax.set_title(f'{label} {ylabel} vs Forecast Horizon')

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
In [23]:
for label in ZONE_LABELS:
    plot_horizon(horizon_results[label], label)

Sampling Frequency¶

In [24]:
def resample_by_interval(df, minutes):
    return df[df.index.minute % minutes == 0].copy()

freq_results = {label: {} for label in ZONE_LABELS}

for freq_name, minutes in FREQUENCIES.items():
    resampled = resample_by_interval(df, minutes)

    for zone, label in zip(ZONES, ZONE_LABELS):
        feat = build_features(resampled, zone, horizon=1)
        X_tr, X_te, y_tr, y_te = split_features(feat, zone)
        model, pred = train_xgb(X_tr, y_tr, X_te, y_te, n_estimators=500, early_stopping_rounds=30)
        freq_results[label][freq_name] = {
            'rmse': rmse(y_te, pred),
            'mape': mape(y_te.values, pred),
            'n_rows': len(resampled),
        }

    print(f'--- {freq_name} done ---')

            for freq, v in results.items()}

})

rows = []
for freq_name in FREQUENCIES:
    row = {'Frequency': freq_name}
    for label in ZONE_LABELS:
        r = freq_results[label][freq_name]
        row[f'{label} MAPE'] = f"{r['mape']:.2f}%"
        row[f'{label} RMSE'] = f"{r['rmse']:.1f}"
    row['Rows'] = f"{freq_results['Zone 1'][freq_name]['n_rows']:,}"
    rows.append(row)

pd.DataFrame(rows).set_index('Frequency')

In [27]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
freq_names = list(FREQUENCIES.keys())

for label, color in zip(ZONE_LABELS, PALETTE):
    mapes = [freq_results[label][f]['mape'] for f in freq_names]
    axes[0].plot(freq_names, mapes, marker='o', color=color, label=label)

axes[0].set_ylabel('MAPE (%)')
axes[0].set_title('Accuracy vs Sampling Frequency')
axes[0].legend()

rows = [freq_results['Zone 1'][f]['n_rows'] for f in freq_names]
axes[1].bar(freq_names, rows, color=PALETTE[0], alpha=0.7)
axes[1].set_ylabel('Dataset Size (rows)')
axes[1].set_title('Data Volume vs Sampling Frequency')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Anomaly Detection¶

In [ ]:
def detect_anomalies(y_test, pred, label, quantile=0.99):
    residuals = y_test - pred
    threshold = residuals.abs().quantile(quantile)
    anomalies = residuals[residuals.abs() > threshold]
    print(f'{label} — Anomaly count: {len(anomalies)}')
    print(anomalies.sort_values(key=abs, ascending=False).head(10))
    return anomalies

anomalies_z1 = detect_anomalies(y_test_z1, pred_z1, 'Zone 1')
anomalies_z2 = detect_anomalies(y_test_z2, pred_z2, 'Zone 2')
anomalies_z3 = detect_anomalies(y_test_z3, pred_z3, 'Zone 3')

In [29]:
def plot_anomalies(y_test, pred, anomalies, label):
    residuals = y_test - pred
    
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 6))
    
    # Actual vs predicted
    axes[0].plot(y_test.index, y_test.values, color=PALETTE[0], linewidth=0.8, label='Actual')
    axes[0].plot(y_test.index, pred, color=PALETTE[1], linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.85, label='Predicted')
    axes[0].scatter(anomalies.index, y_test[anomalies.index], color='red', s=10, zorder=5, label='Anomaly')
    axes[0].set_title(f'{label} — Actual vs Predicted')
    axes[0].legend()
    
    # Residuals
    axes[1].plot(y_test.index, residuals.values, color='grey', linewidth=0.5)
    axes[1].scatter(anomalies.index, anomalies.values, color='red', s=10, zorder=5)
    axes[1].axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
    axes[1].set_title(f'{label} — Residuals')
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_anomalies(y_test_z1, pred_z1, anomalies_z1, 'Zone 1')
plot_anomalies(y_test_z2, pred_z2, anomalies_z2, 'Zone 2')
plot_anomalies(y_test_z3, pred_z3, anomalies_z3, 'Zone 3')

Zone 3 Investigation¶

anomaly_dates = anomalies_z3.index
normal_dates = y_test_z3.index.difference(anomalies_z3.index)

print('Anomaly points:')
print(df.loc[anomaly_dates, ['general diffuse flows', 'Temperature']].describe().round(2))

print('\nNormal points:')
print(df.loc[normal_dates, ['general diffuse flows', 'Temperature']].describe().round(2))

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

axes[0].hist(df.loc[anomaly_dates, 'general diffuse flows'], bins=30, color=PALETTE[1], alpha=0.7, label='Anomaly')
axes[0].hist(df.loc[normal_dates, 'general diffuse flows'], bins=30, color=PALETTE[0], alpha=0.5, label='Normal')
axes[0].set_title('general diffuse flows — Anomaly vs Normal')
axes[0].set_xlabel('general diffuse flows')
axes[0].legend()

axes[1].hist(df.loc[anomaly_dates, 'Temperature'], bins=30, color=PALETTE[1], alpha=0.7, label='Anomaly')
axes[1].hist(df.loc[normal_dates, 'Temperature'], bins=30, color=PALETTE[0], alpha=0.5, label='Normal')
axes[1].set_title('Temperature — Anomaly vs Normal')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Temperature (°C)')
axes[1].legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [32]:
anomaly_counts = pd.DataFrame({
    'Zone 1': anomalies_z1.resample('D').count(),
    'Zone 2': anomalies_z2.resample('D').count(),
    'Zone 3': anomalies_z3.resample('D').count(),
}).fillna(0)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 4))
for col, color in zip(anomaly_counts.columns, PALETTE):
    ax.bar(anomaly_counts.index, anomaly_counts[col], label=col, color=color, alpha=0.7, width=0.8)
ax.set_title('Daily Anomaly Count by Zone')
ax.set_xlabel('Date')
ax.set_ylabel('Count')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
